text.p: Dear Sir,

p.boldface: Invitation to serve as Course Developer

text.p: You have been recommended as a resource person for the development of course materials for the B A English programme of the National Open University of Nigeria. On behalf of the Vice-Chancellor, the School of Arts and Social Sciences therefore wishes to request your expertise in the developing the following course(s):
Course Title: ENG 209: Language and Society
Credit Units: 3 credit units
Details of the exercise are attached as follows:
a) A copy of the course description
b) A copy of 'Basic Principles and Strategies of Course Writing in DDL system'
c) A copy of NOUN house-style format
d) A copy of a sample course guide, and a sample unit
e) A copy of terms of reference for the exercise

The timeline for submission will be 4 weeks from date of acceptance. However, we request that you send in your first completed unit, a course outline (identified topics) within the three days. Please do not hesitate to contact us if you need more information.
Thank you.
Sincerely,
Ph.D
Overseer, School of Arts and Social Sciences
Cc: The Vice-Chancellor
Director, Course Material Development Unit.
text.heading: TERMS OF REFERENCE FOR DEVELOPMENT AND WRITING OF COURSE MATERIALS
text.p: 1.0 All Course materials must be written in NOUN House Style.
1.1 A Course Guide must be provided as a window into what the course is all about: The course guide is the very first study unit but it stands alone.
1.2 A Course must have sufficient number of study units, the minimum is as follows: 1 credit unit * 7 study units; 2 credit units = 14 study units, 3 credit units = 21 study units.
1.3 The study units should be structured Into modules with a module comprising of 4 to 5 related study units.
1.4 Each study unit contains the following subheadings:
1. Title
2 Introduction
3. Objectives
4. Main body arranged in sub-titles and in sequence
6. Conclusion
7. Summary
8. Tutor Marked Assignment
9. References/ Further Reading

1.4.1 As many Self - Assessment Exercises as appropriate should be provided within the body of each unit. Self-assessment exercises are for the student to evaluate his/her understanding of the material so far. They are not to be submitted, However, answers must be provided or directions be given for students as to where to find the answers within the unit.
1.4.2 Each study unit must contain a Tutor Marked Assignment. Answers to all Tutor Marked Assignments and marking Scheme are contained in one separate unit at end of the course material.

1.5 The main body of each unit (that is, excluding the introduction, objectives, conclusion, summary, and references) should be a minimum of 7-10 Printed A4 size pages, using 13-point Times New Roman font size and single spacing. Each study unit must have Self-Assessment Exercises (SAEs) embedded within the unit and at least I Tutor Marked Assignment (TMA) at the end of each study unit.
Note: We realize that units can be of varying depths and as such, no two units will be exactly same in terms of length. However, the above In 1.5 should be viewed as a guide.
1.8 Marking Scheme for the tutor marked assignments must be provided on a stand-alone basis for all.
20 Other Matters.
2.1 A course outline (topics), and the first study unit should be submitted for preview before you continue with the writing.
2.2 Each Study Unit attracts a remuneration of =N=5.000.00.
2.3 Each Developer/Writer will receive one complementary copy of all the publications arising out of their work on the course material.
3.0 Please find attached a sample of a NOUN Course Guide and the first Unit of a NOUN Course material.
4.0 If the terms and conditions are agreeable to you, kindly endorse the letter by appending your signature on the copy attached and return same to the undersigned.
